section.title: Overview
section.para: The rust_credit_check module provides prepaid credit/balance checking with a per-worker &http; cache. It queries an external billing API for account balances, caches results with a configurable TTL, and computes maximum call duration based on the balance and a per-minute rate.
This module is useful for prepaid VoIP services where call authorization depends on available credit.

Key features include:
Debit on call end: POST the call duration and cost to a billing API when a call terminates, using non-blocking fire-and-forget delivery.
Configurable JSON parsing: Extract balance from any JSON response structure using dot-notation field paths (e.g., data.account.balance).
Billing failover: Primary and backup billing URLs with automatic failover and timed recovery.
Per-prefix rate table: Load destination-based rates from a CSV file for accurate per-call duration limits.
Mid-call recheck: Re-query the billing API during long calls to verify remaining credit and adjust call duration limits.
Cache-first async: A cache-optimized variant of credit_check that returns instantly on cache hits, plus optional cache warmup at startup.

section.title: How it works
section.para: When credit_check() is called, the module first checks the per-worker cache for a valid (non-expired) balance entry for the account. On a cache hit, the cached balance is used immediately without any &http; request.
On a cache miss or expiry, the module sends a synchronous &http; GET request to the billing API:
The API must return a JSON response containing the account balance. By default, the module expects a top-level balance field, but this is configurable via the balance_field parameter:

The balance is cached and then evaluated:
balance > 0: call is allowed. Max call duration is computed as (balance / rate_per_min) * 60 seconds.
balance <= 0: call is denied.
On &http; errors or unparseable responses, the on_error parameter controls whether the call is allowed or denied (fail-open vs fail-closed).
If a billing_url_backup is configured and the primary returns a 5xx error, the module automatically fails over to the backup URL. After failover_timeout_secs seconds, the module retries the primary.
The pseudo-variables $var(credit_balance) (string, 2 decimal places) and $var(credit_max_duration) (integer, seconds) are set on every call to credit_check().

section.title: Debit on call end
section.para: When debit_url is configured and debit_on_end is set to 1, the module tracks call start times. When credit_end() is called (typically on BYE), the module computes the call duration and cost, then POSTs a JSON debit record to the billing API:

The debit is sent asynchronously via a fire-and-forget queue, so BYE processing is never delayed by the billing API. If the queue is full, the debit is dropped and logged. For manual control, use credit_debit() to send a debit for an arbitrary account and duration.

section.title: Per-prefix rate table
section.para: When rate_file is configured, the module loads a CSV file containing destination prefix to rate mappings. The file format is:

Lines starting with # are comments. Prefixes are matched longest-first against the destination number. Use credit_check_dest() to specify a destination number for rate lookup. The matched rate is available in $var(credit_rate).

section.title: Mid-call recheck
section.para: For long calls, the operator can set a shorter initial $DLG_timeout and use the dialog on_timeout route to re-check the balance:
credit_recheck() always bypasses the cache, querying the billing API directly for a fresh balance.

section.title: Cache-first async and warmup
section.para: credit_check_async() is optimized for the common case where the cache is warm. On a cache hit, it returns instantly with no &http; request. On a cache miss, it falls back to a blocking query (same as credit_check()).
To ensure the cache is warm from the start, configure cache_warmup_url to an endpoint that returns all account balances as a JSON array:
The warmup URL is queried once per worker at startup. The balance_field path is used to extract the balance from each array element.

section.title: &osips; Modules
section.para:
The following modules must be loaded before this module:
None.

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para:
The following libraries or applications must be installed before running &osips; with this module loaded:
None. &http; client functionality is compiled into the Rust shared library.
An external billing API must be reachable at the configured billing_url.

section.title: billing_url (string)
section.para: The base &url; of the billing/balance API. The module appends ?account={account} to form the full request &url;. This parameter is required. The module will fail to load if this parameter is not set or is empty.

para.emphasis: Default value is None (must be set).

section.title: cache_ttl (integer)
section.para: Time-to-live in seconds for cached balance entries. After this period, the next credit_check() call for the account will trigger a fresh &http; query. Set to 0 to disable caching (not recommended for production).

section.title: http_timeout (integer)
section.para: &http; request timeout in seconds for billing API queries. If the API does not respond within this time, the on_error policy is applied. Must be at least 1; values below 1 are clamped to 1 at module load.

section.title: pool_size (integer)
section.para: Number of &http; connections to maintain per worker process. Connections are reused (keep-alive) for better performance. Must be at least 1; values below 1 are clamped to 1 at module load.

section.title: rate_per_min (integer)
section.para: Default cost units consumed per minute of call time. Used to compute the maximum call duration: max_duration = (balance / rate) * 60 seconds. The result is set in $var(credit_max_duration). Must be at least 1; values below 1 are clamped to 1 at module load. This value is used as the fallback when no prefix match is found in the rate_file.

section.title: on_error (string)
section.para:
Behavior when the billing API is unreachable, returns a non-200 status, or returns unparseable JSON:
deny - reject the call (return -2). Fail-closed.
allow - allow the call (return 1). Fail-open.
Case-insensitive comparison.

section.title: tls_ca_file (string)
section.para: Path to a custom CA certificate file (PEM format) for TLS verification of the billing API endpoint. If not set, the system default CA bundle is used.

section.title: debit_url (string)
section.para: The &url; for debit POST requests. When a call ends and debit_on_end is enabled, the module sends a JSON payload to this &url; with the account, call duration, and computed cost. If not set, no debit is sent.

section.title: debit_on_end (integer)
section.para: Enable automatic call start tracking for debit calculation. When set to 1, credit_check() records the call start time. Call credit_end() on BYE to compute duration and send the debit.

section.title: balance_field (string)
billing_url_backup (string)
section.para: Backup billing API &url;. If the primary billing_url returns a 5xx error or times out, the module automatically switches to this backup. After failover_timeout_secs seconds, the module retries the primary.

section.title: failover_timeout_secs (integer)
section.para: Number of seconds to continue using the backup billing &url; after the primary fails. After this period, the next billing query will retry the primary &url;. Only relevant when billing_url_backup is configured.

section.title: rate_file (string)
section.para: Path to a CSV file containing per-prefix rate mappings. Each line has the format prefix,rate_per_min. Lines starting with # are comments. Prefixes are matched longest-first against the destination number. If no prefix matches, the global rate_per_min is used as fallback.
Use credit_check_dest() to trigger prefix-based rate lookup.

para.emphasis: Default value is None (use global rate).

section.title: recheck_interval_secs (integer)
section.para: Suggested interval in seconds for mid-call balance re-checks. A value of 0 disables re-checking. When greater than 0, the operator should set $DLG_timeout to this value and call credit_recheck() from the on_timeout route to extend the call if credit remains.

section.title: cache_warmup_url (string)
section.para: &url; to pre-load the per-worker cache at startup. The endpoint must return a JSON array of objects with account and the configured balance_field:
Each worker queries this &url; once during child_init. Combined with credit_check_async(), this eliminates blocking &http; requests for the common case.

section.title: publish_events (integer)
section.para: Enable event publishing. When set to 1, the module publishes significant events as NOTICE-level structured log messages that operators can consume via syslog or log aggregation. If the NATS module is loaded, events are also published to NATS subjects under opensips.events.*.
Events published by this module: E_CREDIT_DENIED (when credit check fails) and E_CREDIT_DEBIT (when a debit is posted). Each event includes a JSON payload with relevant details.
Query the billing API (or per-worker cache) for the given account balance. On return, sets $var(credit_balance) and $var(credit_max_duration).
If debit_on_end is enabled and the call is allowed, the call start time is recorded for later debit computation via credit_end().
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to check. Typically $fU (From-&uri; username). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - account has positive credit (call allowed).
-1 (denied) - account has zero or negative credit (call denied).
-2 (error) - &http; error, missing parameter, or uninitialized worker. Subject to on_error policy.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: credit_check usage

section.para: Like credit_check(), but uses the destination number for per-prefix rate lookup. The matched rate is set in $var(credit_rate). Requires a rate_file to be configured for prefix matching; otherwise the global rate_per_min is used.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to check. This parameter is mandatory.
destination (string) - The destination number for rate lookup (typically $rU). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes: same as credit_check().
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: credit_check_dest usage

section.para: Cache-optimized variant of credit_check(). On a cache hit, returns instantly with no &http; request. On a cache miss, falls back to blocking &http;. Combine with cache_warmup_url for best results.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to check. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes: same as credit_check().
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: credit_check_async usage

section.para: Manually trigger a debit POST for the given account and call duration. Computes cost as (duration_secs * rate_per_min / 60) and sends a JSON payload to debit_url. Requires debit_url to be configured.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account to debit. This parameter is mandatory.
duration_secs (integer) - Call duration in seconds. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - debit enqueued for delivery.
-2 (error) - debit_url not configured, queue full, or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

End a tracked call for the given account. Computes the actual call duration from the start time recorded by credit_check() (when debit_on_end is enabled), calculates the cost, and sends a debit POST. If no call is being tracked for this account, returns -1.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account whose call has ended. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - debit enqueued (or logged if debit_url not configured).
-1 - no active call tracked for this account.
-2 (error) - debit queue full or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: credit_end usage

section.para: Re-query the billing API for the account balance, bypassing the cache. Sets $var(credit_balance) and $var(credit_max_duration) with fresh values. Intended for use in dialog on_timeout routes to extend calls when credit remains.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to re-check. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes: same as credit_check().
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: credit_recheck usage

section.para: Clear the cached balance entry for the given account. The next call to credit_check() for this account will trigger a fresh &http; query to the billing API.
This is useful after a call ends to force a balance refresh, or when the billing system signals a balance change.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier whose cache entry should be cleared. This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - cache entry cleared (or was not present).
-2 (error) - missing parameter or uninitialized worker.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: credit_clear usage

section.para: Retrieve module statistics as a JSON string. Sets $var(credit_stats) with a JSON object containing all tracked counters for the current worker process.
Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - always succeeds.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

Retrieve module statistics in Prometheus text exposition format. Sets $var(credit_prom) with a multi-line string containing all counters formatted with # HELP, # TYPE, and metric value lines suitable for scraping by a Prometheus server.
Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - always succeeds.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: credit_prometheus usage
example.programlisting: ... credit_prometheus(); # $var(credit_prom) now contains Prometheus text format ...

section.title: $var(credit_balance)
section.para: The account balance as a string with 2 decimal places (e.g., 42.50 or 0.00). Set by credit_check(), credit_check_dest(), credit_check_async(), and credit_recheck().

section.title: $var(credit_max_duration)
section.para: The maximum call duration in seconds, computed as (balance / rate) * 60. Set as an integer. A value of 0 means no credit available. The rate used depends on whether a prefix match was found in the rate_file.

section.title: $var(credit_rate)
section.para: The per-minute rate used for the max duration computation. Set by credit_check_dest() as a string with 4 decimal places. Useful for logging which rate was applied for a given destination.

section.title: $var(credit_stats)
section.para: JSON string containing the module statistics for the current worker process. Set by credit_stats().

section.title: $var(credit_prom)
section.para: Prometheus text exposition format string containing all module statistics. Set by credit_prometheus().
Total number of credit checks performed.
Number of checks where the account had positive credit.
Number of checks where the account had zero or negative credit.
Number of &http; errors or unparseable responses from the billing API.
Number of balance lookups served from the per-worker cache.
Number of balance lookups that required an &http; query to the billing API.
Number of debit payloads successfully enqueued for delivery.
Number of debit payloads dropped because the queue was full.
Number of times the module switched from primary to backup billing &url;.
Number of mid-call balance re-checks performed via credit_recheck().
